mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals rising inflation, which typically leads to higher interest rates. This reduces the present value of future corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances despite rising CPIAUCSL, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 5%, it signals a weak labor market, prompting central banks to implement monetary easing. This lowers borrowing costs and stimulates equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates robust economic activity and rising demand. This drives corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when both INDPRO and CPIAUCSL are rising, or the interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL has a Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rising housing starts (HOUST) signal overheating in the real estate market, prompting central banks to tighten monetary policy. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of late-cycle expansion periods, or the correlation between HOUST and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strong labor market, boosting consumer spending and corporate revenues. This drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances despite rising PAYEMS, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production (INDPRO) signals increased manufacturing activity, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO is rising, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1200 thousand units, it signals strong demand for residential construction, boosting related industries and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when HOUST exceeds 1200, or the correlation between HOUST and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects robust consumer demand and economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST are rising, or the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST has a Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
